description: Using the loaded 'usa.gdt' dataset, perform an econometric analysis by creating a quarterly GDP growth rate variable (percentage), running an OLS regression of GDP growth on inflation, diagnosing serial correlation with the Durbin-Watson statistic, and applying the Cochrane-Orcutt iterative AR(1) correction. Save the complete Gretl script to '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/cochrane_orcutt.inp' and a report containing the original OLS Durbin-Watson statistic, the estimated AR(1) coefficient (rho), and the corrected coefficient estimates (intercept and inflation slope) to '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/cochrane_orcutt_results.txt'.

metadata.data_source: Principles of Econometrics, 5th Edition (Hill, Griffiths, Lim)
